data.title: Polestar Unleashes Its Biggest EV Blitz Yet
data.description:
Geely Automobile Holdings Ltd.-backed (OTC:GELYF) (OTC:GELHY) Polestar Automotive Holding U.K. PLC (NASDAQ:PSNY) announced on Wednesday that it plans to introduce four new electric vehicles over the next three years, broadening its product portfolio as it targets higher-volume EV segments and seeks to improve operating performance.

  • Polestar Automotive stock is surging to new heights today. Why is PSNY stock up today?

This announcement is part of a broader strategy to enhance its market position as the electric vehicle sector continues to gain traction, with the company aiming to redefine standards in design and sustainability as it competes in a rapidly evolving market.

Polestar has initiated its largest model offensive in history, unveiling plans to introduce four new cars between 2026 and 2028.

Electric Vehicle Lineup Expected By 2028

Financial Performance

Despite a challenging geopolitical and economic environment, Polestar delivered its best year ever in terms of retail sales in 2025.

Retail sales amounted to an estimated 15,608 cars in the fourth quarter of 2025, up 27% year over year. For 2025, retail sales totaled approximately 60,119 cars, a growth of 34%.

In 2026, with a disciplined approach, the company expects to deliver low double-digit retail volume growth.

The sales mix is expected to continue to evolve with an increasing share of Polestar 4.

Further, Polestar aims to focus more on the retail channel expansion of 30%.

Earlier in February, Polestar secured over $400 million in funding through a Special Purpose Vehicle.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 11.76% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 7.47% below its 100-day SMA, suggesting some short-term strength but longer-term challenges. Shares have decreased 49.97% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 46.80, which is considered neutral territory, indicating no strong momentum in either direction. Meanwhile, MACD is above its signal line, suggesting bullish momentum may be building.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating potential for upward movement if market conditions remain favorable.

  • Key Resistance: $20.50
  • Key Support: $17.00

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Polestar is slated to provide its next financial update on March 15, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: -1342 cents (Up from -2910 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $2.99 Billion (Up from $2.03 Billion)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $102.69. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Barclays: Underweight (Raises Target to $15.00) (January 23)
  • Cantor Fitzgerald: Neutral (September 4, 2025)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Gores Guggenheim, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: 3.55 — Stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Gores Guggenheim’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the Momentum (3.55) indicates underperformance, the company’s strategic moves in the EV space suggest potential for recovery.

Top ETF Exposure

  • SPDR S&P Kensho Smart Mobility ETF (NYSE:HAIL): 1.85% Weight

PSNY Price Action: Polestar Automotive shares were up 13.03% at $19.00 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: EXCLUSIVE: La Rosa Acquires Remaining Stake In Florida Brokerage
data.description:
La Rosa Holdings Corp (NASDAQ:LRHC) on Wednesday announced that it has acquired the remaining 49% ownership interest in its franchisee, La Rosa Realty Lakeland LLC (doing business as La Rosa Realty Prestige).

With this transaction, Prestige is now a wholly owned subsidiary of La Rosa.

The financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

Prestige operates in Lakeland, Florida, with an additional branch office in Winter Haven, serving the broader Central Florida market.

Over the last 12 months, Prestige ranked third among real estate brokerages in Polk County, Florida, based on agent count and total real estate sales volume.

Prestige generated approximately $5.1 million in revenue for the trailing twelve months ended September 30, 2025, and reported positive net income during that period.

Joe La Rosa, CEO of La Rosa, noted that Prestige, with 138 agents and 420 transactions completed last year, has significantly contributed to their Central Florida presence.

Strategic Land Acquisition for AI Data Center

Recently, La Rosa signed a contract to purchase a strategically located parcel of land in Osceola County, Central Florida.

The acquisition, set to close on June 15, 2026, is subject to customary closing conditions.

The land acquisition supports the development of a cutting-edge Tier III AI data center to meet increasing demand for high-performance computing and data processing infrastructure.

The 10,000-square-foot facility is expected to support an estimated IT load of ~1,500 kW.

LRHC Price Action: La Rosa Holdings shares were up 5.52% at $1.53 during premarket trading on Wednesday. The stock is trading near its 52-week low of $1.14, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Company

data.title: Bitcoin At $67,000 As Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin Reverse Gains On Regulatory Optimism
data.description:
Bitcoin is holding near $67,000 despite renewed volatility and significant ETF outflows; liquidations stand at $188.97 million over the past 24 hours. 

The backdrop includes optimism surrounding potential progress on the CLARITY Act, which could provide more structured crypto regulatory guidance in the U.S.      

Bitcoin ETFs saw $104.9 million in net outflows on Tuesday, while Ethereum ETFs reported $48.6 million in net inflows.

The meme coin sector is trading relatively flat, slipping 0.9% over the past 24 hours to a total market capitalization of $35.8 billion.

Trader Commentary:

Crypto chart analyst Ali Martinez noted that Bitcoin is consolidating within a tightening triangle structure, signaling price compression. As volatility contracts, the setup implies a potential ~15% move once price breaks decisively in either direction.

Trader Michael van de Poppe added that Bitcoin remains range-bound following the sharp collapse earlier this month. Price action continues to chop without offering clear confirmation for bulls or bears.

Despite the consolidation, some analysts argue Bitcoin's valuation still appears relatively low compared to traditional assets like gold, reinforcing the longer-term macro thesis.

Crypto Tony sees Ethereum pushing toward the $2,030–$2,040 zone, an area likely containing resting liquidity. That region could act as a short-term reaction point. A strong push through would signal continuation, while rejection could reinforce the broader range-bound structure.

Degen Hardy highlighted Solana around the $27 level as a critical decision area. If Bitcoin stabilizes, SOL could present favourable risk/reward for a bounce and it it remains weak, entering aggressively could be risky.

Trader Tardigrade highlights that Dogecoin is forming a classic cup and handle pattern. After bottoming near $0.08 and rallying to around $0.11 to complete the cup, price is now consolidating near the rim as the handle develops.

A confirmed breakout above the handle's resistance could spark continuation momentum and potentially propel DOGE toward new highs.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Tactile Systems Technology Analysts Raise Their Forecasts After Better-Than-Expected Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Tactile Systems reported fourth-quarter earnings of 46 cents, better than the consensus of 44 cents. Sales jumped 21% to $103.59 million, beating the Wall Street estimates of $93.80 million, attributable to lymphedema and airway clearance product lines.

Tactile Systems forecasts fiscal 2026 sales between $357 million-$365 million compared to the consensus of $347.94 million. The company expects adjusted EBITDA in the range of $49 million to $51 million.

Tactile Systems shares fell 5.3% to close at $283.46 on Tuesday.

These analysts made changes to their price targets on Tactile Systems following earnings announcement.

  • BTIG analyst Ryan Zimmerman maintained Tactile Systems Tech with a Buy and raised the price target from $36 to $38.
  • Piper Sandler analyst Adam Maeder maintained the stock with an Overweight rating and raised the price target from $35 to $42.
